OpenAITtsProviderConfig.description:
Per-instance TTS model_provider configuration (`[tts_providers.<type>.<alias>]`).

Mirrors `ModelProviderConfig` in shape — one struct holds the union of
fields across backends. Only the fields relevant to the selected backend
(determined by the outer `<type>` map key) are read at runtime; others
are quietly ignored.

api_key.description: API key (openai, elevenlabs, google).

binary_path.description: Path to backend binary (edge-tts subprocess; piper local server).

model.description:
Model name. OpenAI uses this for `tts-1`/`tts-1-hd`; elevenlabs uses
it as the model_id (e.g. `eleven_monolingual_v1`).

response_format.description:
Audio response format sent to the TTS backend (e.g. `"opus"`, `"mp3"`,
`"wav"`). Defaults to `"opus"` for the OpenAI family. Override to
`"wav"` for Orpheus-class models (e.g. `canopylabs/orpheus-v1-english`
on Groq) or `"mp3"` for broader compatibility.

uri.description:
Endpoint URI for HTTP-based backends. Overrides the family default
when pointing at a compatible third-party API (Groq, Azure, self-hosted
proxies). Set to the **full** URL — there is no separate path-suffix
field. Renamed from `api_url` for parity with `ModelProviderConfig.uri`.

voice.description:
Voice override for this instance. When empty, falls back to
`[tts].default_voice`.

api_key.description: OpenAI API key for Whisper transcription.

model.description: Whisper model name (default: "whisper-1").

OpenAiTranscriptionProviderConfig.description:
Shared base for cloud transcription providers. Each cloud family
composes this via `#[serde(flatten)] base: TranscriptionProviderConfig`.

api_key.description: API key for the transcription provider.

language.description:
Optional language hint passed to the provider (ISO-639-1 like `"en"` /
`"ru"`, or BCP-47 like `"en-US"` for Google). Most providers auto-detect
when this is unset.

model.description: Whisper model name (default: `"whisper-1"`).

OpenCodeCliConfig.description:
OpenCode CLI tool configuration (`[opencode_cli]` section).

Delegates coding tasks to the `opencode run` CLI. Authentication uses the
binary's own session by default — no API key needed unless
`env_passthrough` includes provider-specific keys.

enabled.description: Enable the `opencode_cli` tool

env_passthrough.description: Extra env vars passed to the opencode subprocess

max_output_bytes.description: Maximum output size in bytes (2MB default)

timeout_secs.description: Maximum execution time in seconds (coding tasks can be long)

api_key.description: Secret API token for this model_provider. Grab it from the model_provider's dashboard (OpenAI platform, Anthropic console, OpenRouter keys page, etc.). Stored via the OS keyring when possible; never commit it to config.toml directly.

OpenVpnTunnelConfig.description:
OpenVPN tunnel configuration (`[tunnel.openvpn]`).

Required when `tunnel.tunnel_provider = "openvpn"`. Omitting this section entirely
preserves previous behavior. Setting `tunnel.tunnel_provider = "none"` (or removing
the `[tunnel.openvpn]` block) cleanly reverts to no-tunnel mode.

Defaults: `connect_timeout_secs = 30`.

advertise_address.description:
Advertised address once VPN is connected (e.g., `"10.8.0.2:42617"`).
When omitted the tunnel falls back to `http://{local_host}:{local_port}`.

auth_file.description: Optional path to auth credentials file (`--auth-user-pass`).

config_file.description: Path to `.ovpn` configuration file (must not be empty).

connect_timeout_secs.description: Connection timeout in seconds (default: 30, must be > 0).

extra_args.description: Extra openvpn CLI arguments forwarded verbatim.

api_key.description: Secret API token for this model_provider. Grab it from the model_provider's dashboard (OpenAI platform, Anthropic console, OpenRouter keys page, etc.). Stored via the OS keyring when possible; never commit it to config.toml directly.

chat_template_kwargs.description:
Arbitrary key/value pairs forwarded verbatim as `chat_template_kwargs`
in the request body (llama.cpp-specific). Use this to pass model-family
template variables that control behaviour not exposed by other fields.
Example (Qwen3 thinking suppression):
  `chat_template_kwargs = { enable_thinking = false }`

extra_headers.description: Extra HTTP headers sent with every request. Niche: used for auth bridges, corporate proxies, or custom gateways that demand a tracing header. Most users never touch this; edit `config.toml` directly if you need it.

fallback.description:
Ordered list of other provider aliases to try when every model on this
alias has failed. Each entry is a dotted `<type>.<alias>` reference into
`providers.models` and resolves with its own credentials, endpoint, and
model. A fallback never inherits this alias's key. The walk is
depth-first: this alias's models are exhausted first, then each fallback
alias is descended in turn (applying its own `fallback_models` and
`fallback`). Empty means no provider-level fallback.

fallback_models.description:
Ordered alternate models to try on THIS provider before falling over to
the `fallback` aliases. Same endpoint, key, and headers as the primary
`model`. Only the model identifier changes. Use this when a provider
serves a backup model (e.g. a smaller or older variant) that should be
tried before leaving the provider entirely. Empty means only `model` is
tried.

kind.description:
Provider implementation to instantiate for this profile. Use this
when a canonical typed slot should run through a compatible
implementation, e.g. `[providers.models.openai.proxy] kind =
"openai-compatible"`.

max_tokens.description: Hard cap on response length in tokens. Most models enforce sensible built-in limits already; leave unset unless you specifically need to clip long outputs for cost or latency reasons.

merge_system_into_user.description: ModelProvider-specific quirk: fold the system prompt into the first user message instead of sending a separate system role. Only needed for models that reject (or mishandle) a standalone system role, e.g. certain older Mistral variants.

model.description: Model identifier to send with each request: the ID string from the model_provider's catalog (e.g. `gpt-4o`, `claude-sonnet-4-5`, `llama-3.3-70b`). Must match a model the model_provider actually serves on this account.

native_tools.description:
Override the provider's default for native tool calling.
`None` (default) honors the provider's built-in choice. `Some(true)`
forces native tool calls on, `Some(false)` forces text-fallback.
Currently consulted only by the Groq factory, which defaults to
text-fallback because llama-family Groq models reject native tool
calls with HTTP 400. Setting `native_tools = true` re-enables native
tool calling for Groq models that support it.

pricing.description:
Per-model pricing for cost tracking, USD per 1M tokens.

Free-form key/value map. Keys are user-defined model identifiers; an
optional `.input` / `.output` suffix encodes pricing dimension when
the operator wants to split rates. A bare key without a suffix is
used as a flat per-token rate when neither dimension is specified.
Default is empty: cost tracking falls back to "unknown" rates and
only token usage is recorded.

Example: `pricing = { opus = 15.0, sonnet = 3.0 }`
Or split: `pricing = { "opus.input" = 15.0, "opus.output" = 75.0 }`

provider_extra.description:
Extra JSON parameters to include in API requests.
Merged at the top level of the request body, allowing provider-specific
features (routing, transforms, etc.) without code changes.
Example: `provider_extra = { model_provider = { only = ["Anthropic"] } }`

replay_assistant_reasoning.description:
Whether stored assistant reasoning should be replayed on outbound
assistant history messages. `Some(false)` strips `reasoning_content`
and `reasoning` before sending. `None` (default) honours the provider's
built-in default (true for most compat providers, false for Groq).

requires_openai_auth.description: When true, the client pulls credentials from `OPENAI_API_KEY` or `~/.codex/auth.json` instead of the `api_key` field above. Turn on only for the OpenAI Codex model_provider; leave off for standard API-key model_providers.

temperature.description:
Sampling temperature passed to the model. Lower values (0.0–0.3) give
deterministic, near-verbatim output, which fits code, routing, summarization.
Higher values (0.7–1.2) give more varied output, which fits open-ended chat.

think.description:
Enable or disable chain-of-thought thinking for models that support it
(e.g. Qwen3, GLM-4). `true` turns thinking on, `false` turns it off.
`None` (default) lets the model decide. Forwarded as `enable_thinking`
in the request body; mirrors the Ollama provider's `think` field.

timeout_secs.description: HTTP request timeout in seconds. Bump this for slow local model_providers (Ollama on CPU, big local models) or high-latency networks; leave unset otherwise.

tls_ca_cert_path.description:
Path to a PEM-encoded CA certificate for TLS connections to this provider.
Must be an absolute path; shell expansion (e.g. `~`) is not performed.
Leave unset to use the system's default trust store.

uri.description: Endpoint URI the client hits. Override the family's default endpoint when pointing at a self-hosted gateway (LiteLLM, vLLM, Ollama), a custom proxy, or any non-standard URL. Leave unset to use the family's default URI from its `ModelEndpoint` impl. Set this to the FULL endpoint URL; there is no separate path-suffix field.

wire_api.description: Wire protocol flavor: `responses` for OpenAI's Codex/Responses API, `chat_completions` for everything else (OpenAI chat, Anthropic, OpenRouter, Groq, local gateways). Auto-selected per model_provider; only override if you're forcing an unusual combination.

cache_valid_secs.description: Reuse window for recently validated OTP codes.

challenge_max_attempts.description: Maximum number of OTP challenge attempts before lockout.

enabled.description: Enable OTP gating. Defaults to disabled for backward compatibility.

gated_actions.description:
Tool/action names gated by OTP. Empty or malformed entries are rejected
at config load; an entry that does not match a known gated action is
accepted but logged at WARN, since it cannot be enforced.

gated_domains.description: Explicit domain patterns gated by OTP.

oneOf.description: Time-based one-time password (RFC 6238).
Future method for paired-device confirmations.
Future method for local CLI challenge prompts.

OutputModality.description:
Preferred output modality for a peer group.

Controls how the agent delivers replies to peers in this group when no
stronger per-turn signal is present. `Mirror` (default) preserves the
existing input-driven behaviour: voice in → voice out, text in → text out.

oneOf.description: Always reply in kind — voice note if user sent voice, text otherwise.

Always deliver via TTS as a voice note, regardless of input modality.
Applies to proactive messages (cron, announces) as well as replies.
Always deliver as text, even if user sent a voice note.

PacingConfig.description:
Pacing controls for slow/local LLM workloads (`[pacing]` section).

All fields are optional and default to values that preserve existing
behavior. When set, they extend — not replace — the existing timeout
and loop-detection subsystems.

loop_detection_enabled.description:
Enable pattern-based loop detection (exact repeat, ping-pong,
no-progress). Defaults to `true`.

loop_detection_max_repeats.description:
Number of consecutive identical tool+args calls before the first
escalation (Warning). Defaults to 3.

loop_detection_min_elapsed_secs.description:
Minimum elapsed seconds before loop detection activates.
Tasks completing under this threshold get aggressive loop protection;
longer-running tasks receive a grace period before the detector starts
counting. `None` means loop detection is always active (existing behavior).

loop_detection_window_size.description:
Sliding window size for the pattern-based loop detector.
Defaults to 20.

loop_ignore_tools.description:
Tool names excluded from identical-output / alternating-pattern loop
detection. Useful for browser workflows where `browser_screenshot`
structurally resembles a loop even when making progress.

message_timeout_scale_max.description:
Override for the hardcoded timeout scaling cap (default: 4).
The channel message timeout budget is computed as:
  `message_timeout_secs * min(max_tool_iterations, message_timeout_scale_max)`
Raising this value lets long multi-step tasks with slow local models
receive a proportionally larger budget without inflating the base timeout.

step_timeout_secs.description:
Per-step timeout in seconds: the maximum time allowed for a single
LLM inference turn, independent of the total message budget.
`None` means no per-step timeout (existing behavior).

code_ttl_secs.description: Time-to-live for pending pairing codes in seconds (default: 3600)

lockout_secs.description: Lockout duration in seconds after max attempts (default: 300)

max_failed_attempts.description: Maximum failed pairing attempts before lockout (default: 5)

PeerGroupConfig.description: `[peer_groups.<name>]` — mutual-opt-in peer group on a channel type.

channel.description:
Either a channel type (`"telegram"`) or a dotted channel alias
(`"telegram.work"`). A bare type applies to every alias of that
type; a dotted form scopes the group to that single instance.

ignore.description: Per-group blocklist; subtracts from the resolved peer set.

output_modality.description:
Preferred output modality for all peers in this group.
Defaults to `mirror` (input-driven). Set to `voice` to have the
agent always reply and deliver proactive messages (cron, announces)
as TTS voice notes on channels that support audio output.

PeerUsername.description:
Reference to a configured `[channels.peers.<type>.<alias>]` entry.

Empty value means "no preference" (opt-out). Non-empty values must
resolve to a configured alias; `Config::validate()` enforces this.

PeripheralBoardConfig.description: Configuration for a single peripheral board (e.g. STM32, RPi GPIO).

PeripheralsConfig.description:
Peripheral board integration configuration (`[peripherals]` section).

Boards become agent tools when enabled.

datasheet_dir.description:
Path to datasheet docs (relative to workspace) for RAG retrieval.
Place .md/.txt files named by board (e.g. nucleo-f401re.md, rpi-gpio.md).

enabled.description: Enable peripheral support (boards become agent tools)

api_key.description: Secret API token for this model_provider. Grab it from the model_provider's dashboard (OpenAI platform, Anthropic console, OpenRouter keys page, etc.). Stored via the OS keyring when possible; never commit it to config.toml directly.

region.description: Server region: `"us"` (USA), `"eu"` (Europe), `"ap"` (Asia), `"br"` (South America), `"au"` (Australia), or omit for auto.

token.description: Pinggy access token (optional — free tier works without one).

allowed_tools.description:
Tools allowed in pipeline steps. Steps referencing tools not on this
list are rejected before execution.

enabled.description:
Enable the `execute_pipeline` meta-tool.
Default: `false`.

max_steps.description:
Maximum number of steps allowed in a single pipeline invocation.
Default: `20`.

PiperTtsProviderConfig.description:
Per-instance TTS model_provider configuration (`[tts_providers.<type>.<alias>]`).

Mirrors `ModelProviderConfig` in shape — one struct holds the union of
fields across backends. Only the fields relevant to the selected backend
(determined by the outer `<type>` map key) are read at runtime; others
are quietly ignored.

api_key.description: API key (openai, elevenlabs, google).

binary_path.description: Path to backend binary (edge-tts subprocess; piper local server).

model.description:
Model name. OpenAI uses this for `tts-1`/`tts-1-hd`; elevenlabs uses
it as the model_id (e.g. `eleven_monolingual_v1`).

response_format.description:
Audio response format sent to the TTS backend (e.g. `"opus"`, `"mp3"`,
`"wav"`). Defaults to `"opus"` for the OpenAI family. Override to
`"wav"` for Orpheus-class models (e.g. `canopylabs/orpheus-v1-english`
on Groq) or `"mp3"` for broader compatibility.

uri.description:
Endpoint URI for HTTP-based backends. Overrides the family default
when pointing at a compatible third-party API (Groq, Azure, self-hosted
proxies). Set to the **full** URL — there is no separate path-suffix
field. Renamed from `api_url` for parity with `ModelProviderConfig.uri`.

voice.description:
Voice override for this instance. When empty, falls back to
`[tts].default_voice`.

PluginEntryConfig.description:
Per-plugin config section keyed by plugin alias; values are secret so they
encrypt at rest under the same adjacent `.secret_key` as every other secret.

PluginSecurityConfig.description:
Plugin signature verification configuration (`[plugins.security]`).

Controls Ed25519 signature verification for plugin manifests.
In `strict` mode, only plugins signed by a trusted publisher key are loaded.
In `permissive` mode, unsigned or untrusted plugins produce warnings but are
still loaded. In `disabled` mode (the default), no signature checking occurs.

trusted_publisher_keys.description: Hex-encoded Ed25519 public keys of trusted plugin publishers.

auto_discover.description: Auto-discover and load plugins on startup

enabled.description: Enable the plugin system (default: false)

max_plugins.description: Maximum number of plugins that can be loaded

PostgresStorageConfig.description:
PostgreSQL storage backend (`[storage.postgres.<alias>]`).

Holds connection parameters AND pgvector settings on one alias-keyed
entry; previously these lived in two separate sections.

db_url.description:
Connection URL (e.g. `"postgres://user:pass@host/db"`).
Accepts legacy aliases: dbURL, database_url, databaseUrl.

schema.description: Database schema for the memory table.

vector_enabled.description: Enable pgvector extension for hybrid vector+keyword recall.

default_language.description: Default report language (en, de, fr, it). Default: "en".

enabled.description: Enable the project_intel tool. Default: false.

include_git_data.description: Include git log data in reports. Default: true.

include_jira_data.description: Include Jira data in reports. Default: false.

jira_base_url.description: Jira instance base URL (required if include_jira_data is true).

risk_sensitivity.description: Risk detection sensitivity: low, medium, high. Default: "medium".

ProviderCostRates.description:
`[cost.rates.providers.*]` — provider-shaped rate sheets. Each field
here mirrors a corresponding field on `[providers.*]` with the
trailing alias segment replaced by the resource the rate prices.
The inner typed wrappers carry the per-provider-type slot layout
and own dispatch (their slot list is the single source of truth,
shared with their providers counterpart via the `for_each_*_provider_slot!`
macros in [`crate::providers`]).

Providers.description:
Top-level wrapper for every provider category. TOML root sees a
single `[providers]` table with one sub-key per category:

```toml
[providers.models.anthropic.default]
api_key = "..."

[providers.tts.openai.default]
api_key = "..."

[providers.transcription.groq.default]
api_key = "..."
```

Each category keeps its own typed-slot internals (so per-family
endpoints and extras stay validated at the type level); this
wrapper just gives them a shared top-level home.

ProxyConfig.description: Proxy configuration for outbound HTTP/HTTPS/SOCKS5 traffic (`[proxy]` section).

all_proxy.description: Fallback proxy URL for all schemes.

enabled.description: Enable proxy support for selected scope.

http_proxy.description: Proxy URL for HTTP requests (supports http, https, socks5, socks5h).

https_proxy.description: Proxy URL for HTTPS requests (supports http, https, socks5, socks5h).

no_proxy.description: No-proxy bypass list. Same format as NO_PROXY.

ProxyScope.description: Proxy application scope — determines which outbound traffic uses the proxy.

oneOf.description: Use system environment proxy variables only.
Apply proxy to all ZeroClaw-managed HTTP traffic (default).
Apply proxy only to explicitly listed service selectors.

QQConfig.description: QQ Official Bot configuration (Tencent QQ Bot SDK)

app_id.description: App ID from QQ Bot developer console

app_secret.description: App Secret from QQ Bot developer console

enabled.description:
Whether this channel is active. The runtime only loads channels whose
`enabled = true`. Default: `false` so an operator who pastes a partial
`[channels.<type>.<alias>]` block doesn't accidentally bring a channel
live before the rest of its config is filled in.

excluded_tools.description:
Tools excluded from this channel's tool spec. When set, these tools
are not exposed to the model when responding via this channel.

proxy_url.description:
Per-channel proxy URL (http, https, socks5, socks5h).
Overrides the global `[proxy]` setting for this channel only.

QdrantStorageConfig.description:
Qdrant vector database backend (`[storage.qdrant.<alias>]`).

URL, collection, and API key all fall back to environment variables
(`QDRANT_URL`, `QDRANT_COLLECTION`, `QDRANT_API_KEY`) when unset.

api_key.description:
API key for Qdrant Cloud or secured instances.
Falls back to `QDRANT_API_KEY` env var if unset.

collection.description:
Collection name for storing memories.
Falls back to `QDRANT_COLLECTION` env var, or `"zeroclaw_memories"`.

url.description:
Qdrant server URL (e.g. `"http://localhost:6333"`).
Falls back to `QDRANT_URL` env var if unset.

api_key.description: Secret API token for this model_provider. Grab it from the model_provider's dashboard (OpenAI platform, Anthropic console, OpenRouter keys page, etc.). Stored via the OS keyring when possible; never commit it to config.toml directly.

chat_template_kwargs.description:
Arbitrary key/value pairs forwarded verbatim as `chat_template_kwargs`
in the request body (llama.cpp-specific). Use this to pass model-family
template variables that control behaviour not exposed by other fields.
Example (Qwen3 thinking suppression):
  `chat_template_kwargs = { enable_thinking = false }`

extra_headers.description: Extra HTTP headers sent with every request. Niche: used for auth bridges, corporate proxies, or custom gateways that demand a tracing header. Most users never touch this; edit `config.toml` directly if you need it.

fallback.description:
Ordered list of other provider aliases to try when every model on this
alias has failed. Each entry is a dotted `<type>.<alias>` reference into
`providers.models` and resolves with its own credentials, endpoint, and
model. A fallback never inherits this alias's key. The walk is
depth-first: this alias's models are exhausted first, then each fallback
alias is descended in turn (applying its own `fallback_models` and
`fallback`). Empty means no provider-level fallback.

fallback_models.description:
Ordered alternate models to try on THIS provider before falling over to
the `fallback` aliases. Same endpoint, key, and headers as the primary
`model`. Only the model identifier changes. Use this when a provider
serves a backup model (e.g. a smaller or older variant) that should be
tried before leaving the provider entirely. Empty means only `model` is
tried.

kind.description:
Provider implementation to instantiate for this profile. Use this
when a canonical typed slot should run through a compatible
implementation, e.g. `[providers.models.openai.proxy] kind =
"openai-compatible"`.

max_tokens.description: Hard cap on response length in tokens. Most models enforce sensible built-in limits already; leave unset unless you specifically need to clip long outputs for cost or latency reasons.

merge_system_into_user.description: ModelProvider-specific quirk: fold the system prompt into the first user message instead of sending a separate system role. Only needed for models that reject (or mishandle) a standalone system role, e.g. certain older Mistral variants.

model.description: Model identifier to send with each request: the ID string from the model_provider's catalog (e.g. `gpt-4o`, `claude-sonnet-4-5`, `llama-3.3-70b`). Must match a model the model_provider actually serves on this account.

native_tools.description:
Override the provider's default for native tool calling.
`None` (default) honors the provider's built-in choice. `Some(true)`
forces native tool calls on, `Some(false)` forces text-fallback.
Currently consulted only by the Groq factory, which defaults to
text-fallback because llama-family Groq models reject native tool
calls with HTTP 400. Setting `native_tools = true` re-enables native
tool calling for Groq models that support it.

pricing.description:
Per-model pricing for cost tracking, USD per 1M tokens.

Free-form key/value map. Keys are user-defined model identifiers; an
optional `.input` / `.output` suffix encodes pricing dimension when
the operator wants to split rates. A bare key without a suffix is
used as a flat per-token rate when neither dimension is specified.
Default is empty: cost tracking falls back to "unknown" rates and
only token usage is recorded.

Example: `pricing = { opus = 15.0, sonnet = 3.0 }`
Or split: `pricing = { "opus.input" = 15.0, "opus.output" = 75.0 }`

provider_extra.description:
Extra JSON parameters to include in API requests.
Merged at the top level of the request body, allowing provider-specific
features (routing, transforms, etc.) without code changes.
Example: `provider_extra = { model_provider = { only = ["Anthropic"] } }`

replay_assistant_reasoning.description:
Whether stored assistant reasoning should be replayed on outbound
assistant history messages. `Some(false)` strips `reasoning_content`
and `reasoning` before sending. `None` (default) honours the provider's
built-in default (true for most compat providers, false for Groq).

requires_openai_auth.description: When true, the client pulls credentials from `OPENAI_API_KEY` or `~/.codex/auth.json` instead of the `api_key` field above. Turn on only for the OpenAI Codex model_provider; leave off for standard API-key model_providers.

temperature.description:
Sampling temperature passed to the model. Lower values (0.0–0.3) give
deterministic, near-verbatim output, which fits code, routing, summarization.
Higher values (0.7–1.2) give more varied output, which fits open-ended chat.

think.description:
Enable or disable chain-of-thought thinking for models that support it
(e.g. Qwen3, GLM-4). `true` turns thinking on, `false` turns it off.
`None` (default) lets the model decide. Forwarded as `enable_thinking`
in the request body; mirrors the Ollama provider's `think` field.

timeout_secs.description: HTTP request timeout in seconds. Bump this for slow local model_providers (Ollama on CPU, big local models) or high-latency networks; leave unset otherwise.

tls_ca_cert_path.description:
Path to a PEM-encoded CA certificate for TLS connections to this provider.
Must be an absolute path; shell expansion (e.g. `~`) is not performed.
Leave unset to use the system's default trust store.

uri.description: Endpoint URI the client hits. Override the family's default endpoint when pointing at a self-hosted gateway (LiteLLM, vLLM, Ollama), a custom proxy, or any non-standard URL. Leave unset to use the family's default URI from its `ModelEndpoint` impl. Set this to the FULL endpoint URL; there is no separate path-suffix field.

wire_api.description: Wire protocol flavor: `responses` for OpenAI's Codex/Responses API, `chat_completions` for everything else (OpenAI chat, Anthropic, OpenRouter, Groq, local gateways). Auto-selected per model_provider; only override if you're forcing an unusual combination.

QueryClassificationConfig.description:
Automatic query classification — classifies user messages by keyword/pattern
and routes to the appropriate model hint. Disabled by default.

enabled.description: Enable automatic query classification. Default: `false`.

rules.description: Classification rules evaluated in priority order.

QwenEndpoint.description: Qwen endpoint variants. Operators pick the region matching their account.

oneOf.description: Mainland China (DashScope).
International (alicloud international).
United States (DashScope US).
Code-specialist endpoint.

QwenModelProviderConfig.description:
Qwen model model_provider config. Multi-region (`endpoint` required) and
supports both API key and OAuth flows (`auth_mode` chooses which).

api_key.description: Secret API token for this model_provider. Grab it from the model_provider's dashboard (OpenAI platform, Anthropic console, OpenRouter keys page, etc.). Stored via the OS keyring when possible; never commit it to config.toml directly.

auth_mode.description:
Auth flow. Defaults to `api_key`; set to `oauth` to use the vendor's
OAuth-cache integration instead of the `api_key` field.

oauth_client_id.description:
Override of Qwen's published OAuth client_id. Most operators
should leave this unset.

oauth_refresh_token.description:
Long-lived Qwen OAuth refresh token. When set, the runtime
exchanges it for a short-lived access token at provider
construction time. Operators relying on the upstream `qwen login`
tool (which writes `~/.qwen/oauth_creds.json`) leave this unset;
the file-cache integration takes over.

oauth_resource_url.description:
Operator override of the resource URL the refreshed access token
is paired with. When unset, the runtime falls back to the
`endpoint`-derived URL (or the cached `resource_url` when reading
from `~/.qwen/oauth_creds.json`).

refresh_token.description: Reddit OAuth2 refresh token for persistent access.

subreddits.description:

from any subreddit the bot has access to. Migrated from the legacy

ReliabilityConfig.description:
Reliability and supervision configuration (`[reliability]` section).

Controls model_provider retries, API key rotation, and channel restart backoff.

api_keys.description:
Additional API keys for round-robin rotation on rate-limit (429) errors.
The primary `api_key` is always tried first; these are extras.

provider_backoff_ms.description: Base backoff (ms) for model_provider retry delay.

scheduler_retries.description: Max retries for cron job execution attempts.

RiskProfileConfig.description:
Named risk/autonomy profile (`[risk_profiles.<alias>]`).

Unified policy surface. Agents reference a profile by alias and the
runtime resolves through it for shell command allowlists, approval gates,
sandbox/resource limits, and delegation guardrails. The conventional
`risk_profiles["default"]` is the resolution target for non-agent
contexts (orchestrator init, cron worker startup); the `Default` impl
below mirrors the legacy safety-first defaults so a fresh install
behaves the same as a config from before the per-profile split.

allowed_commands.description: Allowlist of executable names for shell execution.

allowed_roots.description: Extra directory roots the agent may access.

allowed_tools.description:
Tools the agent may call in agentic mode. Empty = inherit / no
authorization constraint. Authorization decision: which tools is
the agent permitted to invoke at all. See `excluded_tools` for
the inverse denylist scoped to non-CLI channels.

The TOML config does not distinguish an omitted field from
`allowed_tools = []`; both deserialize to `Vec::new()` and
`SecurityPolicy::from_profiles` maps that to "no authorization
constraint" at this layer. If you need an explicit deny-all gate,
apply it on the caller-supplied per-run `allowed_tools` (cron
jobs and other narrowers pass that list in directly to
`ToolAccessPolicy`, which honors `Some(vec![])` as deny-all) or
via `excluded_tools` covering the specific tools you want blocked.

MCP exception: when the list is non-empty, runtime-discovered MCP
tools (any name containing `__`, which is the `<server>__<tool>`
convention used by the MCP wrapper) are auto-admitted into the
effective allow-list without needing to be listed here individually.
This keeps the post-#7464 eager-MCP default usable for agents with an
explicit allow-list. Block individual MCP tools via `excluded_tools`.

Scope of the exception: the `__` auto-admit applies only to this
risk-profile allow-list, **not** to caller-supplied per-run
`allowed_tools` (cron job `allowed_tools`, narrowed delegate
invocations, etc.). Per-run lists are still strict explicit-list
intersections, so a job that narrows `allowed_tools = ["cron_add"]`
will not see runtime-discovered MCP tools unless it names them.
See PR #7547 review for the rationale.

always_ask.description: Tools that always require approval in this profile.

auto_approve.description: Tools that never require approval in this profile.

block_high_risk_commands.description: Block high-risk commands even when allowlisted.

delegation_policy.description:
Whether and to which agents this profile may delegate. Defaults to
`Forbidden`. Delegation requires caller and target to share a risk
profile; the allow-list names the reachable same-profile agents.

excluded_tools.description:
Tools excluded from non-CLI channels under this profile.

Also subtracts from the agentic-delegate allow-list resolved at
runtime, which is the only way to block individual
`<server>__<tool>` MCP names that would otherwise be auto-admitted
by the `allowed_tools` MCP exception described above.

firejail_args.description: Extra arguments forwarded to firejail when sandbox_backend = "firejail".

level.description: Autonomy level applied to this profile. Default: `supervised`.

sandbox_enabled.description: Whether the sandbox is enabled for this profile. `None` inherits global.

shell_env_passthrough.description: Environment variable names passed through to shell subprocesses.

workspace_only.description: Restrict filesystem access to workspace-relative paths. Default: `false`.

RiskProfileRef.description:
Reference to a configured `[risk_profiles.<type>.<alias>]` entry.

Empty value means "no preference" (opt-out). Non-empty values must
resolve to a configured alias; `Config::validate()` enforces this.

kind.description: Runtime kind: native | docker | cloudflare.

reasoning_effort.description: Optional reasoning effort for model_providers that expose a level control.

reasoning_enabled.description:
Global reasoning override for model_providers that expose explicit controls.
- `None`: model_provider default behavior
- `Some(true)`: request reasoning/thinking when supported
- `Some(false)`: disable reasoning/thinking when supported

RuntimeProfileConfig.description:
Named runtime/LLM execution profile (`[runtime_profiles.<alias>]`).

Reusable operational tuning: agentic mode, iteration caps, context
budget, parallel dispatch, resource ceilings, recursion depth, and
the budget knobs that `SecurityPolicy` enforces with subagent
parent-subset discipline. Anything authorization-shaped (allowed
commands/tools/paths, approval gates, sandbox) lives on
`[risk_profiles.<alias>]`. Anything model-provider shaped (model,
temperature, max_tokens, timeout_secs) lives on
`[providers.models.<type>.<alias>]`.

agentic_timeout_secs.description: Agentic delegate run timeout in seconds. `None` inherits global.

compact_context.description: Use compact bootstrap (6000 chars / 2 RAG chunks). `None` inherits.

delegation_timeout_secs.description: Delegate call timeout in seconds. `None` inherits global delegate timeout.

keep_tool_context_turns.description: Number of recent turns whose full tool context is preserved. `None` inherits.

max_actions_per_hour.description:
Maximum actions allowed per hour. `0` is a hard zero budget — the
per-sender rate tracker treats a max of 0 as always exhausted
(`PerSenderTracker::is_exhausted`), blocking every action. For an
effectively-unlimited budget use a high value (e.g. `u32::MAX`), not 0.
`SecurityPolicy::ensure_no_escalation_beyond` rejects subagents
that try to raise this above the parent's value.

max_context_tokens.description: Maximum estimated tokens for context before compaction. `None` inherits.

max_cost_per_day_cents.description:
Maximum cost per day in cents. `0` inherits the global limit.
Parent-subset enforced for subagents.

max_delegation_depth.description: Maximum delegation recursion depth. `0` inherits the default.

max_history_messages.description: Maximum conversation history messages retained per session. `None` inherits.

max_system_prompt_chars.description: Maximum characters for the assembled system prompt. `None` inherits.

max_tool_iterations.description: Maximum tool-call iterations in agentic mode. `0` inherits the global default.

max_tool_result_chars.description: Maximum characters for a single tool result. `None` inherits.

memory_recall_limit.description:
Maximum memory entries injected per turn. `None` inherits global default (5).
Set to `0` for unlimited.

parallel_tools.description: Enable parallel tool execution per iteration. `None` inherits.

shell_timeout_secs.description:
Shell subprocess timeout in seconds. `0` inherits the global timeout.
Parent-subset enforced for subagents.

tool_call_dedup_exempt.description: Tools exempt from within-turn dedup check.

RuntimeProfileRef.description:
Reference to a configured `[runtime_profiles.<type>.<alias>]` entry.

Empty value means "no preference" (opt-out). Non-empty values must
resolve to a configured alias; `Config::validate()` enforces this.

api_key.description: Secret API token for this model_provider. Grab it from the model_provider's dashboard (OpenAI platform, Anthropic console, OpenRouter keys page, etc.). Stored via the OS keyring when possible; never commit it to config.toml directly.

chat_template_kwargs.description:
Arbitrary key/value pairs forwarded verbatim as `chat_template_kwargs`
in the request body (llama.cpp-specific). Use this to pass model-family
template variables that control behaviour not exposed by other fields.
Example (Qwen3 thinking suppression):
  `chat_template_kwargs = { enable_thinking = false }`

extra_headers.description: Extra HTTP headers sent with every request. Niche: used for auth bridges, corporate proxies, or custom gateways that demand a tracing header. Most users never touch this; edit `config.toml` directly if you need it.

fallback.description:
Ordered list of other provider aliases to try when every model on this
alias has failed. Each entry is a dotted `<type>.<alias>` reference into
`providers.models` and resolves with its own credentials, endpoint, and
model. A fallback never inherits this alias's key. The walk is
depth-first: this alias's models are exhausted first, then each fallback
alias is descended in turn (applying its own `fallback_models` and
`fallback`). Empty means no provider-level fallback.

fallback_models.description:
Ordered alternate models to try on THIS provider before falling over to
the `fallback` aliases. Same endpoint, key, and headers as the primary
`model`. Only the model identifier changes. Use this when a provider
serves a backup model (e.g. a smaller or older variant) that should be
tried before leaving the provider entirely. Empty means only `model` is
tried.

kind.description:
Provider implementation to instantiate for this profile. Use this
when a canonical typed slot should run through a compatible
implementation, e.g. `[providers.models.openai.proxy] kind =
"openai-compatible"`.

max_tokens.description: Hard cap on response length in tokens. Most models enforce sensible built-in limits already; leave unset unless you specifically need to clip long outputs for cost or latency reasons.

merge_system_into_user.description: ModelProvider-specific quirk: fold the system prompt into the first user message instead of sending a separate system role. Only needed for models that reject (or mishandle) a standalone system role, e.g. certain older Mistral variants.

model.description: Model identifier to send with each request: the ID string from the model_provider's catalog (e.g. `gpt-4o`, `claude-sonnet-4-5`, `llama-3.3-70b`). Must match a model the model_provider actually serves on this account.

native_tools.description:
Override the provider's default for native tool calling.
`None` (default) honors the provider's built-in choice. `Some(true)`
forces native tool calls on, `Some(false)` forces text-fallback.
Currently consulted only by the Groq factory, which defaults to
text-fallback because llama-family Groq models reject native tool
calls with HTTP 400. Setting `native_tools = true` re-enables native
tool calling for Groq models that support it.

pricing.description:
Per-model pricing for cost tracking, USD per 1M tokens.

Free-form key/value map. Keys are user-defined model identifiers; an
optional `.input` / `.output` suffix encodes pricing dimension when
the operator wants to split rates. A bare key without a suffix is
used as a flat per-token rate when neither dimension is specified.
Default is empty: cost tracking falls back to "unknown" rates and
only token usage is recorded.

Example: `pricing = { opus = 15.0, sonnet = 3.0 }`
Or split: `pricing = { "opus.input" = 15.0, "opus.output" = 75.0 }`

provider_extra.description:
Extra JSON parameters to include in API requests.
Merged at the top level of the request body, allowing provider-specific
features (routing, transforms, etc.) without code changes.
Example: `provider_extra = { model_provider = { only = ["Anthropic"] } }`

replay_assistant_reasoning.description:
Whether stored assistant reasoning should be replayed on outbound
assistant history messages. `Some(false)` strips `reasoning_content`
and `reasoning` before sending. `None` (default) honours the provider's
built-in default (true for most compat providers, false for Groq).

requires_openai_auth.description: When true, the client pulls credentials from `OPENAI_API_KEY` or `~/.codex/auth.json` instead of the `api_key` field above. Turn on only for the OpenAI Codex model_provider; leave off for standard API-key model_providers.

temperature.description:
Sampling temperature passed to the model. Lower values (0.0–0.3) give
deterministic, near-verbatim output, which fits code, routing, summarization.
Higher values (0.7–1.2) give more varied output, which fits open-ended chat.

think.description:
Enable or disable chain-of-thought thinking for models that support it
(e.g. Qwen3, GLM-4). `true` turns thinking on, `false` turns it off.
`None` (default) lets the model decide. Forwarded as `enable_thinking`
in the request body; mirrors the Ollama provider's `think` field.

timeout_secs.description: HTTP request timeout in seconds. Bump this for slow local model_providers (Ollama on CPU, big local models) or high-latency networks; leave unset otherwise.

tls_ca_cert_path.description:
Path to a PEM-encoded CA certificate for TLS connections to this provider.
Must be an absolute path; shell expansion (e.g. `~`) is not performed.
Leave unset to use the system's default trust store.

uri.description: Endpoint URI the client hits. Override the family's default endpoint when pointing at a self-hosted gateway (LiteLLM, vLLM, Ollama), a custom proxy, or any non-standard URL. Leave unset to use the family's default URI from its `ModelEndpoint` impl. Set this to the FULL endpoint URL; there is no separate path-suffix field.

wire_api.description: Wire protocol flavor: `responses` for OpenAI's Codex/Responses API, `chat_completions` for everything else (OpenAI chat, Anthropic, OpenRouter, Groq, local gateways). Auto-selected per model_provider; only override if you're forcing an unusual combination.

SchedulerConfig.description:
Scheduler configuration for periodic task execution (`[scheduler]` section).

Owns the cron-runtime knobs: per-job declarations live on
`Config.cron: HashMap<String, CronJobDecl>` (alias-keyed), while the
scheduler loop's runtime behavior (`enabled`, polling cap, catch-up) lives here.

catch_up_on_startup.description:
Run all overdue jobs at scheduler startup. Default: `true`.

When the daemon restarts late, jobs whose `next_run` is in the past
fire once before normal polling resumes. Disable to wait for the
next scheduled occurrence instead.

enabled.description: Enable the built-in scheduler loop. When false, no cron jobs run.

max_concurrent.description: Maximum tasks executed in parallel within a single polling cycle.

max_run_history.description: Maximum number of historical cron run records to retain. Default: `50`.

max_tasks.description: Maximum number of persisted scheduled tasks per polling cycle.

oneOf.description: Pure keyword search (FTS5 BM25)
Pure vector/semantic search
Weighted combination of keyword + vector (default)

encrypt.description: Enable encryption for API keys and tokens at rest

SecurityConfig.description:
Security configuration for audit logging, OTP, e-stop, IAM/SSO, and WebAuthn.

Sandbox backend and resource limits live on per-agent risk profiles
(see `RiskProfileConfig::sandbox_*` and `RiskProfileConfig::max_*`); the
runtime resolves them via `Config::active_risk_profile(agent_alias)`.

nevis.description: Nevis IAM integration for SSO/MFA authentication and role-based access.

otp.description: OTP gating configuration for sensitive actions/domains.

webauthn.description: WebAuthn / FIDO2 hardware key authentication configuration.

SecurityOpsConfig.description: Managed Cybersecurity Service (MCSS) dashboard agent configuration (`[security_ops]`).

auto_triage.description: Automatically triage incoming alerts without user prompt.

enabled.description: Enable security operations tools.

max_auto_severity.description:
Maximum severity level that can be auto-remediated without approval.
One of: "low", "medium", "high", "critical". Default: "low".

playbooks_dir.description: Directory containing incident response playbook definitions (JSON).

require_approval_for_actions.description: Require human approval before executing playbook actions.

siem_integration.description: Optional SIEM webhook URL for alert ingestion.

api_key.description: Secret API token for this model_provider. Grab it from the model_provider's dashboard (OpenAI platform, Anthropic console, OpenRouter keys page, etc.). Stored via the OS keyring when possible; never commit it to config.toml directly.

chat_template_kwargs.description:

ShellToolConfig.description:
Shell tool configuration (`[shell_tool]` section).

Controls the behaviour of the `shell` execution tool. The main
tunable is `timeout_secs` — the maximum wall-clock time a single
shell command may run before it is killed.

timeout_secs.description: Maximum shell command execution time in seconds (default: 60).

account.description: E.164 phone number of the signal-cli account (e.g. "+1234567890").

approval_timeout_secs.description: Seconds to wait for operator approval on `always_ask` tools before auto-denying.

dm_only.description:
When true, only accept direct messages and ignore all group traffic.
Mutually exclusive with `group_ids` (which is ignored when this is
set). Migrated from the legacy `group_id = "dm"` sentinel.

enabled.description:
Whether this channel is active. The runtime only loads channels whose
`enabled = true`. Default: `false` so an operator who pastes a partial
`[channels.<type>.<alias>]` block doesn't accidentally bring a channel
live before the rest of its config is filled in.

excluded_tools.description:
Tools excluded from this channel's tool spec. When set, these tools
are not exposed to the model when responding via this channel.

group_ids.description:
Group IDs to filter messages. Empty = accept all messages (DMs and
groups). When non-empty, only messages from listed groups are
accepted (DMs are still accepted unless `dm_only` flips the policy
to DMs-only). Migrated from the legacy `group_id` singular field.

http_url.description: Base URL for the signal-cli HTTP daemon (e.g. `"http://127.0.0.1:8686"`).

ignore_attachments.description: Skip messages that are attachment-only (no text body).

proxy_url.description:
Per-channel proxy URL (http, https, socks5, socks5h).
Overrides the global `[proxy]` setting for this channel only.

reply_min_interval_secs.description:
Per-(channel, recipient) outbound pacing floor in seconds.
Range: `0..=REPLY_MIN_INTERVAL_MAX_SECS` (0 disables).

reply_queue_depth_max.description:
Per-(channel, recipient) outbound pacing queue depth.
Range: `0..=REPLY_QUEUE_DEPTH_CEILING`. When `reply_min_interval_secs > 0`
and this value is `0`, the pacing wrapper substitutes
`DEFAULT_REPLY_QUEUE_DEPTH` (16). When the queue is full, the
newest send is dropped and a `WARN` is logged.

api_key.description: Secret API token for this model_provider. Grab it from the model_provider's dashboard (OpenAI platform, Anthropic console, OpenRouter keys page, etc.). Stored via the OS keyring when possible; never commit it to config.toml directly.

SkillBundleConfig.description:
Named skill bundle (`[skill_bundles.<alias>]`).

A reusable group of skills that can be attached to an agent or channel
by alias, controlling which skills are loaded and from where.

directory.description: Directory path (relative to workspace root) to load skills from.

exclude.description: Skill names to exclude from this bundle.

include.description: Skill names to include. Empty means include all skills in `directory`.

enabled.description:
Enable automatic skill creation after successful multi-step tasks.
Default: `false`.

max_skills.description:
Maximum number of auto-generated skills to keep.
When exceeded, the oldest auto-generated skill is removed (LRU eviction).

similarity_threshold.description:
Embedding similarity threshold for deduplication.
Skills with descriptions more similar than this value are skipped.

SkillImprovementConfig.description:
Skill self-improvement configuration (`[skills.skill-improvement]` section).

Controls the post-turn background review fork that may patch, expand, or
archive skills based on what the conversation revealed. The fork runs in a
restricted toolset (only `skills_list`, `skill_view`, `skill_manage`) and
never touches the user-visible conversation.

cooldown_secs.description:
Minimum interval (in seconds) between reviews for the same skill.
Acts as a durable rate limit on patches to any one skill. Default: `3600`.

enabled.description:
Enable the background skill-review fork. Default: `false`.
Opt-in: users must explicitly enable this to allow post-turn skill
mutations.

max_review_iterations.description:
Maximum tool-call iterations the review fork itself is allowed to make.
Caps the fork's LLM cost. Default: `8`.

nudge_interval_iterations.description:
Spawn a review fork once at least this many tool-call iterations have
accumulated in the current run. `0` disables iteration-based triggering.
Default: `10`.

enabled.description:
Enable suggestions for installable skills before normal agent turns.
Default: `false`.

allow_scripts.description:
Allow script-like files in skills (`.sh`, `.bash`, `.ps1`, shebang shell files).
Default: `false` (secure by default).

extra_registries.description:
Additional user-configured skill registries, installed via
`registry:<name>/<skill>`. Each reuses the git-clone registry path and
is cloned to its own `extra-registry-<name>/` workspace directory.

install_suggestions.description: Prompt-triggered install suggestions for missing skills.

open_skills_dir.description:
Optional path to a local open-skills repository.
If unset, defaults to `$HOME/open-skills` when enabled.

open_skills_enabled.description:
Enable loading and syncing the community open-skills repository.
Default: `false` (opt-in).

prompt_injection_mode.description:
Controls how skills are injected into the system prompt.
`full` preserves legacy behavior. `compact` keeps context small and loads skills on demand.

registry_url.description:
URL of the skills registry repository for bare-name installs.
Default: `https://github.com/zeroclaw-labs/zeroclaw-skills`

skill_creation.description: Autonomous skill creation from successful multi-step task executions.

skill_improvement.description: Automatic skill self-improvement after successful skill usage.

oneOf.description: Inline full skill instructions and tool metadata into the system prompt.
Inline only compact skill metadata (name/description/location) and load details on demand.

app_token.description:
Slack app-level token for Socket Mode (xapp-...). When unset or empty,
resolved at channel construction from `ZEROCLAW_SLACK_APP_TOKEN`, then
`SLACK_APP_TOKEN`. `#[serde(default)]` makes omission explicit (an
`Option` field is already omittable, but this keeps it consistent with
`bot_token`).

approval_timeout_secs.description: Seconds to wait for operator approval on `always_ask` tools before auto-denying.

bot_token.description:
Slack bot OAuth token (xoxb-...). Optional in config: when unset or
empty it is resolved at channel construction from
`ZEROCLAW_SLACK_BOT_TOKEN`, then `SLACK_BOT_TOKEN`. `#[serde(default)]`
so a config that omits it still deserializes - the env fallback then
supplies it - instead of failing with `missing field 'bot_token'`.
See #6844 / #6237.

cancel_reaction.description:
Emoji reaction name (without colons) that cancels an in-flight request.
For example, `"x"` means reacting with `:x:` cancels the task.
Leave unset to disable reaction-based cancellation.

channel_ids.description:
Explicit list of channel IDs to watch.
Empty = listen across all accessible channels.
Migrated from the legacy `channel_id` singular field.

draft_update_interval_ms.description: Minimum interval (ms) between draft message edits to avoid Slack rate limits.

enabled.description:
Whether this channel is active. The runtime only loads channels whose
`enabled = true`. Default: `false` so an operator who pastes a partial
`[channels.<type>.<alias>]` block doesn't accidentally bring a channel
live before the rest of its config is filled in.

excluded_tools.description:
Tools excluded from this channel's tool spec. When set, these tools
are not exposed to the model when responding via this channel.

interrupt_on_new_message.description:
When true, a newer Slack message from the same sender in the same channel
cancels the in-flight request and starts a fresh response with preserved history.

mention_only.description:
When true, only respond to messages that @-mention the bot in groups.
Direct messages remain allowed.

proxy_url.description:
Per-channel proxy URL (http, https, socks5, socks5h).
Overrides the global `[proxy]` setting for this channel only.

reply_min_interval_secs.description:
Per-(channel, recipient) outbound pacing floor in seconds.
Range: `0..=REPLY_MIN_INTERVAL_MAX_SECS` (0 disables).

reply_queue_depth_max.description:
Per-(channel, recipient) outbound pacing queue depth.
Range: `0..=REPLY_QUEUE_DEPTH_CEILING`. When `reply_min_interval_secs > 0`
and this value is `0`, the pacing wrapper substitutes
`DEFAULT_REPLY_QUEUE_DEPTH` (16). When the queue is full, the
newest send is dropped and a `WARN` is logged.

stream_drafts.description: Enable progressive draft message streaming via `chat.update`.

strict_mention_in_thread.description:
When true (and `mention_only` is also true), messages inside a Slack
thread must also @-mention the bot to trigger a response. By default,
thread replies are allowed through without a mention so the bot can
keep a back-and-forth going without the user repeating @-mentions.
Set this to true in channels shared with human discussion where the
bot should stay silent unless explicitly addressed.

thread_replies.description:
When true (default), replies stay in the originating Slack thread.
When false, replies go to the channel root instead.

use_markdown_blocks.description:
Use the newer Slack `markdown` block type (12 000 char limit, richer formatting).
Defaults to false (uses universally supported `section` blocks with `mrkdwn`).
Enable this only if your Slack workspace supports the `markdown` block type.

SlashCommandScope.description:
Where a channel registers its skill slash commands. `global` (default)
registers application-wide - the commands work everywhere the bot is, but
Discord takes up to ~1h to propagate changes. `guild` registers to each
guild in `guild_ids`, which propagates instantly (the right choice for fast
iteration and single-server bots). When `guild` is set with an empty
`guild_ids`, the channel logs a warning and falls back to global.

oneOf.description: Application-wide commands (propagate in up to ~1h). Default.
Commands registered to each guild in `guild_ids` (instant propagation).

SopConfig.description:
Standard Operating Procedures engine configuration (`[sop]`).

The `default_execution_mode` field uses the `SopExecutionMode` type from
`sop::types` (re-exported via `sop::SopExecutionMode`). To avoid circular
module references, config stores it using the same enum definition.

approval_timeout_secs.description:
Approval timeout in seconds. When a run waits for approval longer than
this, Critical/High-priority SOPs auto-approve; others stay waiting.
Set to 0 to disable timeout.

default_execution_mode.description:
Default execution mode for SOPs that omit `execution_mode`.
Values: `auto`, `supervised` (default), `step_by_step`,
`priority_based`, `deterministic`.

max_concurrent_total.description: Maximum total concurrent SOP runs across all SOPs.

max_finished_runs.description:
Maximum number of finished runs kept in memory for status queries.
Oldest runs are evicted when over capacity. 0 = unlimited.

persist_runs.description:
Persist run state durably across restarts. Default `false` keeps today's
ephemeral in-memory behavior (no surprise activation on upgrade). When set
to `true`, `build_sop_engine` selects the configured backend and in-flight
runs survive a restart.

run_state_dir.description:
Directory for the durable run store (created mode-0700). When omitted,
`<data_dir>/sop`. Never OS-temp.

run_store_backend.description:
Durable run-state backend when `persist_runs` is true: `sqlite` (default,
durable) or `memory` (explicitly non-durable, for tests/degraded).

sops_dir.description:
Directory containing SOP definitions (subdirs with SOP.toml + SOP.md).
Required to enable runtime SOP loading. When omitted, no SOPs are loaded
at runtime; CLI commands (`sop list`, `sop validate`, `sop show`) still
resolve the default `<workspace>/sops` for offline inspection.

SopRunStoreBackend.description:
Durable SOP run-state backend selector. A closed, compile-time-known set, so it
is a serde enum rather than free text (mirrors `SandboxBackend` /
`ObservabilityBackend` in this file); unknown values are rejected at parse time.

oneOf.description: Durable WAL-mode SQLite store (default).
Ephemeral in-memory store: explicitly non-durable, for tests / degraded boot.

open_timeout_secs.description:
Maximum seconds to wait when opening the DB if it's locked.
`None` waits indefinitely. Recommended max: 300.

path.description:
Optional override for the SQLite database path.
When unset, defaults to `<workspace_dir>/brain.db`.

oneOf.description: Mainland China endpoint.
International endpoint.

api_key.description: Secret API token for this model_provider. Grab it from the model_provider's dashboard (OpenAI platform, Anthropic console, OpenRouter keys page, etc.). Stored via the OS keyring when possible; never commit it to config.toml directly.

chat_template_kwargs.description:
Arbitrary key/value pairs forwarded verbatim as `chat_template_kwargs`
in the request body (llama.cpp-specific). Use this to pass model-family
template variables that control behaviour not exposed by other fields.
Example (Qwen3 thinking suppression):
  `chat_template_kwargs = { enable_thinking = false }`

extra_headers.description: Extra HTTP headers sent with every request. Niche: used for auth bridges, corporate proxies, or custom gateways that demand a tracing header. Most users never touch this; edit `config.toml` directly if you need it.

fallback.description:
Ordered list of other provider aliases to try when every model on this
alias has failed. Each entry is a dotted `<type>.<alias>` reference into
`providers.models` and resolves with its own credentials, endpoint, and
model. A fallback never inherits this alias's key. The walk is
depth-first: this alias's models are exhausted first, then each fallback
alias is descended in turn (applying its own `fallback_models` and
`fallback`). Empty means no provider-level fallback.

fallback_models.description:
Ordered alternate models to try on THIS provider before falling over to
the `fallback` aliases. Same endpoint, key, and headers as the primary
`model`. Only the model identifier changes. Use this when a provider
serves a backup model (e.g. a smaller or older variant) that should be
tried before leaving the provider entirely. Empty means only `model` is
tried.

kind.description:
Provider implementation to instantiate for this profile. Use this
when a canonical typed slot should run through a compatible
implementation, e.g. `[providers.models.openai.proxy] kind =
"openai-compatible"`.

max_tokens.description: Hard cap on response length in tokens. Most models enforce sensible built-in limits already; leave unset unless you specifically need to clip long outputs for cost or latency reasons.

merge_system_into_user.description: ModelProvider-specific quirk: fold the system prompt into the first user message instead of sending a separate system role. Only needed for models that reject (or mishandle) a standalone system role, e.g. certain older Mistral variants.

model.description: Model identifier to send with each request: the ID string from the model_provider's catalog (e.g. `gpt-4o`, `claude-sonnet-4-5`, `llama-3.3-70b`). Must match a model the model_provider actually serves on this account.

native_tools.description:
Override the provider's default for native tool calling.
`None` (default) honors the provider's built-in choice. `Some(true)`
forces native tool calls on, `Some(false)` forces text-fallback.
Currently consulted only by the Groq factory, which defaults to
text-fallback because llama-family Groq models reject native tool
calls with HTTP 400. Setting `native_tools = true` re-enables native
tool calling for Groq models that support it.

pricing.description:
Per-model pricing for cost tracking, USD per 1M tokens.

Free-form key/value map. Keys are user-defined model identifiers; an
optional `.input` / `.output` suffix encodes pricing dimension when
the operator wants to split rates. A bare key without a suffix is
used as a flat per-token rate when neither dimension is specified.
Default is empty: cost tracking falls back to "unknown" rates and
only token usage is recorded.

Example: `pricing = { opus = 15.0, sonnet = 3.0 }`
Or split: `pricing = { "opus.input" = 15.0, "opus.output" = 75.0 }`

provider_extra.description:
Extra JSON parameters to include in API requests.
Merged at the top level of the request body, allowing provider-specific
features (routing, transforms, etc.) without code changes.
Example: `provider_extra = { model_provider = { only = ["Anthropic"] } }`

replay_assistant_reasoning.description:
Whether stored assistant reasoning should be replayed on outbound
assistant history messages. `Some(false)` strips `reasoning_content`
and `reasoning` before sending. `None` (default) honours the provider's
built-in default (true for most compat providers, false for Groq).

requires_openai_auth.description: When true, the client pulls credentials from `OPENAI_API_KEY` or `~/.codex/auth.json` instead of the `api_key` field above. Turn on only for the OpenAI Codex model_provider; leave off for standard API-key model_providers.

temperature.description:
Sampling temperature passed to the model. Lower values (0.0–0.3) give
deterministic, near-verbatim output, which fits code, routing, summarization.
Higher values (0.7–1.2) give more varied output, which fits open-ended chat.

think.description:
Enable or disable chain-of-thought thinking for models that support it
(e.g. Qwen3, GLM-4). `true` turns thinking on, `false` turns it off.
`None` (default) lets the model decide. Forwarded as `enable_thinking`
in the request body; mirrors the Ollama provider's `think` field.

timeout_secs.description: HTTP request timeout in seconds. Bump this for slow local model_providers (Ollama on CPU, big local models) or high-latency networks; leave unset otherwise.

tls_ca_cert_path.description:
Path to a PEM-encoded CA certificate for TLS connections to this provider.
Must be an absolute path; shell expansion (e.g. `~`) is not performed.
Leave unset to use the system's default trust store.

uri.description: Endpoint URI the client hits. Override the family's default endpoint when pointing at a self-hosted gateway (LiteLLM, vLLM, Ollama), a custom proxy, or any non-standard URL. Leave unset to use the family's default URI from its `ModelEndpoint` impl. Set this to the FULL endpoint URL; there is no separate path-suffix field.

wire_api.description: Wire protocol flavor: `responses` for OpenAI's Codex/Responses API, `chat_completions` for everything else (OpenAI chat, Anthropic, OpenRouter, Groq, local gateways). Auto-selected per model_provider; only override if you're forcing an unusual combination.

StorageConfig.description:
Persistent storage configuration (`[storage]` section).

Storage is a two-tier alias-keyed map: `[storage.<backend>.<alias>]`,
parallel to `[providers.models.<type>.<alias>]`. Each backend has its own typed
config struct. `MemoryConfig.backend` carries a dotted reference (`"sqlite.default"`,
`"postgres.work"`) that resolves to one of these entries via
[`Config::resolve_active_storage`].

StreamMode.description: Streaming mode for channels that support progressive message updates.

oneOf.description: No streaming -- send the complete response as a single message (default).
Update a draft message with every flush interval.
Send the response as multiple separate messages at paragraph boundaries.

funnel.description: Use Tailscale Funnel (public internet) vs Serve (tailnet only)

ack_reactions.description:
Override for the top-level `ack_reactions` setting. When `None`, the
channel falls back to `[channels].ack_reactions`. When set
explicitly, it takes precedence.

api_base_url.description:
Telegram Bot API base URL. Defaults to the official Telegram endpoint;
set to a local Bot API server URL when self-hosting Telegram's bot API.

approval_timeout_secs.description:
How long (seconds) to wait for the operator to tap an inline-keyboard
button on a tool approval prompt before auto-denying. Default: 120.

draft_update_interval_ms.description: Minimum interval (ms) between draft message edits to avoid rate limits.

enabled.description:
Whether this channel is active. The runtime only loads channels whose
`enabled = true`. Default: `false` so an operator who pastes a partial

excluded_tools.description:
Tools excluded from this channel's tool spec. When set, these tools
are not exposed to the model when responding via this channel.

interrupt_on_new_message.description:
When true, a newer Telegram message from the same sender in the same chat
cancels the in-flight request and starts a fresh response with preserved history.

mention_only.description:
When true, only respond to messages that @-mention the bot in groups.
Direct messages are always processed.

proxy_url.description:
Per-channel proxy URL (http, https, socks5, socks5h).

stream_mode.description: Streaming mode for progressive response delivery via message edits.

uri.description: Endpoint URI the client hits. Override the family's default endpoint when pointing at a self-hosted gateway (LiteLLM, vLLM, Ollama), a custom proxy, or any non-standard URL.
